section: Chapter 3: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: What is a linear equation in two variables?
questions.correct_answer: An equation of the form ax + by + c = 0, where a and b are not both zero
questions.explanation: It represents a straight line in the coordinate plane.

questions.question: What does the graph of a linear equation represent?
questions.explanation: All solutions of the equation lie on the straight line.

questions.question: What is the solution of a pair of linear equations graphically?
questions.correct_answer: The point of intersection of the two lines
questions.explanation: The common point satisfies both equations.

questions.question: When does a pair of linear equations have no solution?
When does a pair of linear equations have infinitely many solutions?
Which method solves equations by eliminating one variable?
questions.explanation: One variable is removed by adding or subtracting equations.

questions.question: Which method replaces one variable using the other equation?
questions.explanation: One equation is substituted into the other.

questions.question: What condition represents parallel lines?
questions.explanation: Slopes are equal but intercepts differ.

questions.question: What type of system has exactly one solution?
What is a consistent pair of equations?
questions.correct_answer: A pair having at least one solution

notes.title: Graphical Interpretation
notes.content: Solutions of linear equations can be visualised using graphs.

notes.title: Nature of Solutions
notes.content: The nature depends on the relative positions of the lines.

notes.title: Algebraic Methods
notes.content: Substitution, elimination and cross-multiplication are algebraic methods.

notes.title: Consistency
notes.content: Consistency tells whether solutions exist or not.

notes.title: Real-Life Applications
notes.content: Many word problems can be solved using pairs of linear equations.

facts.fact: Coincident lines represent the same equation.
